mission.title: external-secrets: Support Bitwarden Secrets Manager
mission.description: Support Bitwarden Secrets Manager. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support Bitwarden Secrets Manager

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Bitwarden Secrets Manager is currently not supported by external-secrets.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Bitwarden recently re

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Bitwarden Secrets Manager
steps.description:
## Type of change

```

- [ x] New feature development

```

## Objective

The objective is to develop a Golang wrapper for the Bitwarden client that interacts with a native rust library. This is to ensure that a consistent interface is provided, and the details of the Bitwarden rust library can be 
```yaml
curl -X POST https://vault.bitwarden.com/identity/connect/token -H "content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" --data 'scope=api.secrets&client_id=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx&client_secret=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX&grant_type=client_credentials'


{"access_token":"<TOKEN>","expires_in":3600,"token_type":"Bearer","scope":"api.secrets","encrypted_payload":"<PAYLOAD>"}
```

steps.title: Upgrade external-secrets to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade external-secrets:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade external-secrets jetstack/external-secrets --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm Support Bitwarden Secrets Manager is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Type of change

```

- [ x] New feature development

```

## Objective

The objective is to develop a Golang wrapper for the Bitwarden client that interacts with a native rust library. This is to ensure that a consistent interface is provided, and the details of the Bitwarden rust library can be abstracted away.

## Code changes
BitwardenClient.go

    Defines the BitwardenClient struct with de. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
